Retro Modern Spaceframe

World Cup Calendar Project Summary

Generated at 2026-06-24 22:02:08Z UTC. Current feed contains 54 scheduled matches.

Default theme: Starlight Signal. Accent color is intentionally limited to headings, markers, and signal lines so the page stays anchored in black, white, silver, and grey.

This file is regenerated by python -m world_cup_calendar. If the process changes and the generator runs again, this summary updates with the latest instructions and project state.

How to use the project and files

  • Run python -m world_cup_calendar to regenerate the calendar artifacts.
  • Use the ICS file for publishing or subscription workflows; do not repeatedly import separate copies if you want update-in-place behavior.
  • Subscribe to https://theblackrich.github.io/zealot/world-cup.ics from Outlook, Apple Calendar, or Google Calendar for automatic updates.
  • Open this HTML file in a browser for the current project summary and operating instructions, then compare the preview sibling files if you want to review alternate visual directions.

Artifacts written by the process

ICS outputdist\world-cup.ics
HTML summary outputdist\world-cup-summary.html
State file.state\world-cup-state.json
HTML preview (Eclipse Amber)dist\world-cup-summary-preview-eclipse-amber.html
HTML preview (Ion Rose)dist\world-cup-summary-preview-ion-rose.html
HTML preview (Lunar Lime)dist\world-cup-summary-preview-lunar-lime.html
Published ICS copyC:\Users\thebl\OneDrive\Projects\Zealot\world-cup.ics
Published HTML summary copyC:\Users\thebl\OneDrive\Projects\Zealot\world-cup-summary.html
Published preview (Eclipse Amber)C:\Users\thebl\OneDrive\Projects\Zealot\world-cup-summary-preview-eclipse-amber.html
Published preview (Ion Rose)C:\Users\thebl\OneDrive\Projects\Zealot\world-cup-summary-preview-ion-rose.html
Published preview (Lunar Lime)C:\Users\thebl\OneDrive\Projects\Zealot\world-cup-summary-preview-lunar-lime.html

Current implementation plan

  • Generate the World Cup ICS feed and the HTML summary on every run.
  • Copy the generated artifacts into the local OneDrive project folder on Windows.
  • Publish the ICS file from the live GitHub Pages URL https://theblackrich.github.io/zealot/world-cup.ics for Outlook, Apple Calendar, and Google Calendar subscriptions.
  • Keep deterministic UIDs and sequence tracking so subscribed clients update events instead of duplicating them.

Actions already taken

  • Scaffolded a greenfield Python project in the previously empty repository.
  • Integrated the openfootball/worldcup source dataset and selected the latest tournament directory automatically.
  • Parsed only scheduled fixture lines so the calendar contains upcoming World Cup matches.
  • Merged the group-stage and knockout schedule sources so the feed now spans all upcoming matches through the final.
  • Generated a deterministic ICS feed with stable event UIDs and persisted sequence state.
  • Added scheduled refresh automation for 2 PM, 4 PM, 6 PM, and 10 PM Pacific Time.
  • Configured Windows runs to copy the ICS file into C:\Users\thebl\OneDrive\Projects\Zealot.
  • Published the calendar, landing page, and summary on GitHub Pages at https://theblackrich.github.io/zealot/.
  • Added this HTML summary artifact so usage, plan, and implementation details ship with each run.

Calendar subscription notes

  • Outlook: use Add calendar > Subscribe from web with the final public ICS URL.
  • Apple Calendar: use File > New Calendar Subscription with the final public ICS URL.
  • Google Calendar: use Other calendars > From URL with the final public ICS URL.
  • Repeated file import creates duplicates. Stable subscription URLs plus stable ICS UIDs are what enable update-in-place behavior.
